description:
Jira: TBD (inherits from parent epic mutagen-wrd)

Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex changes:
  - Add optional `:task_sup` to the `@type input` typespec (`optional(:task_sup) => atom() | pid()`). Production defaults to `MutagenEx.TaskSup`.
  - Replace `Task.async(fn -> ... end)` (line ~175) with `Task.Supervisor.async_nolink(task_sup, fn -> ... end)` where `task_sup = Map.get(input, :task_sup, MutagenEx.TaskSup)`. Body inside the closure is unchanged (test_modules add_module loop + try/rescue/catch around `apply(ex_unit, :run, [])`).
  - `Task.yield` call (line ~196): no change. Task.yield speaks monitor; `:DOWN`-vs-`:EXIT` shift is contained.
  - `cancel_on_timeout` (line ~228): thread `task_sup` through as a parameter. Keep `Task.shutdown(task, grace_ms)` for phase 1 (structured-return shape). The `nil` arm calls `brutal_shutdown(task, task_sup)`.
  - `brutal_shutdown` (line ~280): swap `Task.shutdown(task, :brutal_kill)` for `Task.Supervisor.terminate_child(task_sup, task.pid)`. Immediately after `terminate_child`, call `Process.demonitor(task.ref, [:flush])` to prevent stale `:DOWN` accumulation across timeout sites. Preserve the `pre_kill_result = Task.yield(task, 0)` case structure unchanged.
- Per-site task crash path remains: caller observes via Task struct's monitor, arrives at the `{:exit, reason}` case arm in mutation_loop.ex:210-211. No new receive-for-:EXIT anywhere.

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - v2 of mutagen.mutation_pipeline (mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs) green — outcome tuple shapes unchanged
  - v3, v4 of mutagen.mutation_pipeline (mix test test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs, c2_test.exs) green
  - v5 of mutagen.mutation_pipeline (mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs) green
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - Outcome tuple shape for `:timeout` is byte-for-byte identical to pre-`.18` (existing structural assertions in mutation_runner_test.exs continue to hold).
  - r4 (revised) holds: timed-out site classified `:timeout`; next iteration carries `tainted_predecessors: true`; `:code.purge/1` runs on scoped modules before restore.
  - The runner's mailbox stays clean across N consecutive timeout sites (no stale `:DOWN` accumulation). Verifiable via inspection but not a unit-test gate in S2 — S4 scenario 3 + scenario 5 are the structural gates.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (that is S1)
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex (that is S3)
- test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs (that is S4)
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md (that is S5)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex internals — `.17`'s `with_restore/4` MUST NOT be touched or duplicated
- everything else in lib/mutagen_ex/

Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex (only)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not introduce parallelism on the mutation loop — having Task.Supervisor is the foundation; `.30` builds on it.
- Do not change `MutationLoop.run/1`'s outcome shape — tagged tuples MUST stay identical.
- Do not change `cancel_grace_ms` default (100 ms) or `:shutdown_timeout` (5_000 ms). Tuning is `.30`.
- Do not thread `:task_sup` through `MutationRunner.run/1` — injection is at the MutationLoop boundary only (per refined plan TC-12).
- Do not add a separate receive for `:EXIT` info messages — trust `Task.yield/2` / `Task.shutdown/2`'s monitor handling.

Merge gate:
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [ ] mix test green (mutation_runner_test.exs and integration c1/c2 are the regression gate)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket
title: S2: mutation_loop.ex async_nolink + terminate_child swap + demonitor fix
